meta_title: Impact Windows in Hollywood, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Hollywood, FL. HVHZ-certified, 170mph wind-rated. Protect your home & save on insurance. Free quotes!
h1: Hollywood's Trusted Impact Window Installation — Built for the Broadwalk Coast and Beyond
intro:
Hollywood, Florida sits in one of the most storm-exposed corridors in the entire state. With the Atlantic Ocean just 1.5 miles to the east and Broward County's full hurricane history pressing down from the north and south, homeowners from Hollywood Beach to Emerald Hills face a very real and annual threat from tropical weather. The question is no longer whether a major storm will affect Hollywood — it's whether your home will be ready when it does. Impact windows are the single most effective structural upgrade a Hollywood homeowner can make, and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has been making that upgrade seamless, affordable, and fully code-compliant for hundreds of South Florida families.

Hollywood's housing stock tells an important story. More than half of the homes in the city — roughly 55% — were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping changes brought on by Hurricane Andrew. Properties in Hollywood Lakes, Hollywood Hills, and the neighborhoods west of I-95 were constructed in an era when glazing requirements were minimal and storm shutters were considered optional accessories rather than essential safety features. Today, those same homes sit on a real estate market where the average property value hovers around $400,000 — a serious investment that deserves serious protection. Upgrading to impact-resistant windows isn't just a safety decision; it's a financial one that can preserve and grow your home's equity for decades to come.

From the oceanfront condos and single-family homes along Hollywood Beach to the quiet tree-lined streets near ArtsPark at Young Circle and the established communities of Emerald Hills, every neighborhood in Hollywood faces the same core vulnerability: inadequate glazing. Standard windows — even those with storm shutters bolted on — cannot match the consistent, code-compliant protection of permanently installed impact windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing specializes exclusively in this space, bringing certified products, in-house installation crews, and 60+ years of combined experience to every Hollywood home we serve. This page explains everything you need to know about protecting your Hollywood property with impact windows — the right way.

section_why.h2: Why Hollywood Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for the Job
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't subcontract, we don't cut corners, and we don't disappear after the install is done. Every single installation we perform in Hollywood — whether it's a two-bedroom bungalow near Dania Beach or a five-bedroom waterfront home in Hollywood Lakes — is completed by our own in-house team of licensed installers. That distinction matters enormously in a city where fly-by-night contractors often flood the market after hurricane warnings or high-wind events. With an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and more than 500 completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our track record speaks for itself.

Our Hollywood crews understand this city's building culture intimately. We've worked with the City of Hollywood Building Division through dozens of permit cycles, we know what inspectors look for in the HVHZ environment, and we know how to ensure your new windows pass the first time without costly delays or re-inspections. That local knowledge is not something you can replicate by hiring a generic contractor — it's built through years of showing up, pulling permits correctly, and treating every homeowner's project with the same precision we'd apply to our own homes.

We also bring financial accessibility to a market where full-scale window replacement can feel out of reach. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. Our team will walk you through every available option — including potential insurance premium reductions that can offset your monthly payment considerably — so you can make the best decision for your family's budget. We believe that living within 1.5 miles of the South Florida coastline should not mean choosing between financial stability and physical safety.

Finally, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's focus is singular: impact windows and roofing. We don't try to be everything to everyone. That laser focus allows us to source the best HVHZ-rated products, stay current with Florida Building Code updates specific to Broward County, and deliver installations that hold up not just to the building inspector's eye — but to the next Category 4 storm that tests them.

section_history.h2: Hollywood's Hurricane History Makes Impact Windows a Non-Negotiable Investment
section_history.body:
Hollywood's storm history is a sobering reminder of what coastal South Florida endures. Since 1990, the Hollywood area has been significantly impacted by at least 10 named storms or hurricanes. The most defining of these events was Hurricane Andrew in August 1992, a Category 5 storm that redrew the map of South Florida's building codes forever. While Andrew's epicenter was further south in Miami-Dade County, its outer bands delivered destructive wind and rain to Broward County, exposing the catastrophic fragility of pre-code construction. The aftermath of Andrew directly drove the creation of the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) standards that now govern every window installation in Hollywood.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 was particularly punishing for the Hollywood area. Unlike most Atlantic hurricanes, Wilma made landfall on the Gulf Coast and swept across the state from west to east, subjecting Broward County to sustained winds and an unusually prolonged period of storm-force gusts. Many Hollywood homeowners who had relied on accordion shutters or plywood found their protection systems inadequate — windows blew in, roofs lost their edge metal, and interior water damage followed. The financial and emotional toll was severe. Then, in 2017, Hurricane Irma delivered yet another reminder: even a storm that weakens before landfall can produce wind speeds and surge conditions that overwhelm homes without permanent impact glazing.

The pattern is clear and undeniable. Hollywood is not a city that gets to sit on the sidelines during hurricane season. Homes along the Hollywood Beach Broadwalk corridor, properties near the Anne Kolb Nature Center, and neighborhoods tucked into the inland grid between US-1 and I-95 all face meaningful exposure every single year from June through November. Impact windows are the only solution that requires no last-minute preparation, no human error, and no storage of bulky hardware — they are simply always protecting your home, day in and day out.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Florida Building Code Requirements for Hollywood Impact Windows
section_code.body:
Hollywood, Florida falls entirely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), the most demanding building standard classification in the United States. The HVHZ designation — which covers all of Miami-Dade and Broward counties — was established in the wake of Hurricane Andrew and represents the gold standard of wind resistance code for residential construction. Any impact window installed in Hollywood must be tested, rated, and certified to meet HVHZ protocols, which go significantly beyond the standard Florida Building Code requirements applied elsewhere in the state. Products must pass Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) testing and carry the corresponding certification documentation.

Hollywood's designation as a 170mph wind zone means that every window and door opening in a newly renovated or newly constructed home must be protected by products specifically engineered for that wind speed. This is not simply a matter of choosing 'impact-rated' windows from a catalog — it requires selecting products that carry HVHZ-specific approvals and have been installed in strict accordance with the manufacturer's installation documentation. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we only install ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI products, both of which carry the appropriate HVHZ certifications for Broward County installations.

The permit process in Hollywood runs through the City of Hollywood Building Division, and all impact window replacements require a proper permit, product approval documentation, and inspection sign-off before the project is considered legally complete. Homeowners who skip the permit process risk complications during home sales, insurance claims, and future inspections. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to final approval — ensuring your installation is fully documented, legally compliant, and ready to support your insurance wind mitigation report.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: HVHZ-Certified Products Built for Hollywood's Coastal Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact window lines in Hollywood: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands have earned their place in South Florida's most demanding installation environments through rigorous independent testing, HVHZ certification, and a proven track record of performance across Broward County's coastal and inland communities. When we recommend a product to a Hollywood homeowner, it is because that product has been proven — not just rated on paper, but physically tested against the conditions that define life near the Atlantic coast.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) are engineered specifically for the South Florida market. Their impact-resistant laminated glass — featuring a durable interlayer between two panes — holds together on impact rather than shattering, preventing the sudden pressure change that can lift a roof during hurricane-force winds. ESW frames are constructed with multi-chamber profiles that add structural rigidity and thermal efficiency, helping Hollywood homeowners reduce cooling loads during the region's long, humid summers. Low-E glass coatings further block solar heat gain, which is a meaningful benefit in a city that sees intense UV exposure year-round.

CWI impact windows offer complementary performance with a focus on architectural versatility, making them an excellent choice for Hollywood's diverse housing stock — from the mid-century modern homes in Hollywood Hills to the newer construction closer to Pembroke Pines and Hallandale Beach. Both ESW and CWI products are rated to withstand Hollywood's 170mph wind zone requirements, carry Miami-Dade NOA approval, and are available in a range of styles including single-hung, double-hung, casement, picture, and slider configurations. Together, these two product lines allow Premier Impact Windows & Roofing to match the right window to every home's architecture, budget, and performance needs without compromise.

section_cost.h2: What Does Impact Window Installation Cost in Hollywood — and Is It Worth It?
section_cost.body:
For Hollywood homeowners with an average property value of approximately $400,000, impact window installation represents one of the highest-return investments available in the home improvement space. The cost of a full-home impact window replacement in Hollywood typically ranges depending on the number of openings, window styles, glass options, and structural conditions — but the financial equation goes well beyond the upfront price tag. When you factor in insurance premium reductions, elimination of shutter installation costs each season, and the long-term durability of laminated impact glass, the math shifts considerably in your favor.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes that investment more accessible with financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down for qualified applicants. Many Hollywood homeowners are surprised to learn that their monthly insurance savings alone — often between $200 and $500 annually for a properly documented wind mitigation upgrade — can offset a meaningful portion of their monthly financing payment. When you add the elimination of annual shutter rental, purchase, and installation costs, the net out-of-pocket experience of financing impact windows can be remarkably manageable.

Beyond monthly savings, impact windows add measurable resale value to Hollywood homes. Buyers in the Hollywood Beach, Emerald Hills, and Hollywood Lakes markets are increasingly sophisticated about storm protection — and a home with fully permitted, HVHZ-certified impact windows commands attention and offers negotiating leverage in a competitive market. Real estate professionals in Broward County consistently report that impact-protected homes sell faster and with fewer concessions than comparably priced homes still relying on shutters or unrated glazing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will walk you through the full cost picture — installation, financing, savings, and long-term ROI — during your free, no-pressure consultation.

section_insurance.h2: Significant Insurance Savings Await Hollywood Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowners insurance market is one of the most volatile in the country, and Broward County residents feel that volatility acutely. Hollywood homeowners have seen premiums spike in recent years as carriers reassess their exposure along the South Florida coast. However, one of the most powerful tools available to Hollywood homeowners for reducing those premiums is also one of the most permanent: impact windows with a verified wind mitigation inspection.

Under Florida law, insurance carriers are required to offer discounts to homeowners whose properties meet specific wind mitigation standards. Impact windows are among the highest-value credits available on a wind mitigation report. When a licensed inspector certifies that your Hollywood home has impact-rated openings throughout — windows and doors — your carrier must apply the corresponding discount to your premium. For many Hollywood homeowners, these discounts range from 15% to 45% or more on the wind portion of their policy, which is typically the most expensive component in a coastal Broward County market.

Major carriers operating in the Hollywood area — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several private market insurers — all recognize HVHZ-certified impact windows as a premium-reducing feature. The wind mitigation report that documents your new impact windows from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your ticket to those savings, and it remains valid for five years before requiring re-inspection. Homeowners who pair impact windows with a compliant roof system often see compounded savings that make their monthly financing payment nearly self-funding through reduced insurance costs alone. Our team can help you understand what savings to expect before you ever sign a contract — because an informed homeowner is a confident homeowner.

faqs.q: Are impact windows truly necessary in Hollywood, FL, or can I use storm shutters instead?
faqs.a: While storm shutters provide some level of protection, they require manual installation before every storm event — leaving your home vulnerable if a hurricane develops quickly. Hollywood sits 1.5 miles from the Atlantic coast in an HVHZ zone rated for 170mph winds, and impact windows provide year-round, always-on protection that shutters simply cannot match. Impact windows also qualify for insurance discounts that shutters typically do not, making them a smarter long-term investment for Hollywood homeowners.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to install impact windows in a Hollywood, FL home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary depending on the number of openings, window styles, and your home's specific structural needs. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants. Many Hollywood homeowners find that insurance premium reductions and the elimination of annual shutter costs significantly offset the monthly financing payment, making impact window upgrades more affordable than they initially appear.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace windows with impact windows in Hollywood, FL?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact window replacements in Hollywood require a permit from the City of Hollywood Building Division. Because Hollywood is in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), products must also carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) approval, and installations must be inspected before the permit is closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application and inspection coordination process on your behalf, ensuring your project is fully code-compliant from start to finish.

faqs.q: How long does a full impact window installation take in a Hollywood home?
faqs.a: For most single-family homes in Hollywood, the physical installation of impact windows takes one to three days depending on the number of openings and any structural conditions that need to be addressed. The overall project timeline — including permitting, product ordering, and final inspection — typically ranges from four to eight weeks from contract signing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing keeps homeowners informed at every stage and coordinates all city inspections directly with the City of Hollywood Building Division.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance after installing impact windows in Hollywood, FL?
faqs.a: Hollywood homeowners with HVHZ-certified impact windows throughout their home can qualify for wind mitigation insurance credits that reduce premiums by 15% to 45% or more on the wind coverage portion of their policy — which is typically the largest component in coastal Broward County. A licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your new Premier Impact Windows & Roofing windows and submit the report to your carrier. Savings vary by carrier and coverage level, but many homeowners recoup hundreds of dollars annually, making impact windows one of the best ROI upgrades available.

cta_body: From Hollywood Beach to Emerald Hills, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is Hollywood's trusted choice for HVHZ-certified impact window installation — call us today or fill out our quick online form to schedule your free, no-obligation consultation and take the first step toward year-round storm protection.
